title: BeamNetwork Object
description: A geometry reference property that defines a BeamNetwork object which is commonly used in discrete lattice structures.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: create
methods.description: The creator function of the BeamNetwork object.

properties.name: beams
properties.description: The beams of the beam network. Each beam is a pair of vertex indices. The size of the array should be a multiple of 2, and equal to the length of the radii array.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: radii
properties.description: The radii of the beams. Each radius is a double value. The radii define the radius of one end of the beam that corresponds to the same index in the beams array. The size of the array should be a multiple of 2, and equal to the length of the beams array.

properties.name: vertices
properties.description: The vertices of the beam network. Each vertex is a Point3D.

beams.title: BeamNetwork.beams Property
beams.description: The beams of the beam network. Each beam is a pair of vertex indices. The size of the array should be a multiple of 2, and equal to the length of the radii array.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = beamNetwork_var.beams

# Set the value of the property.
beamNetwork_var.beams = propertyValue

isValid.title: BeamNetwork.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = beamNetwork_var.isValid

objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.

radii.title: BeamNetwork.radii Property
radii.description: The radii of the beams. Each radius is a double value. The radii define the radius of one end of the beam that corresponds to the same index in the beams array. The size of the array should be a multiple of 2, and equal to the length of the beams array.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = beamNetwork_var.radii

# Set the value of the property.
beamNetwork_var.radii = propertyValue

vertices.title: BeamNetwork.vertices Property
vertices.description: The vertices of the beam network. Each vertex is a Point3D.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = beamNetwork_var.vertices

# Set the value of the property.
beamNetwork_var.vertices = propertyValue

classType.title: BeamNetwork.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

create.title: BeamNetwork.create Method
create.description: The creator function of the BeamNetwork object.
